message:
perf(mvcc): SSI candidate-free prepare fast-path + publish CAS histogram cleanup

Two related SSI prepare-path optimisations.

1. begin_concurrent.rs: candidate-free prepare fast-path.
   prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi() now short-circuits two
   trivially-no-conflict shapes before the full ActiveEdgeDiscoveryIndex
   + edge-discovery pipeline runs:

   - Both read and write sets empty. The witness-key summaries are
     computed once up front (moved above the first fast-path check),
     and the returned PreparedConcurrentCommit has empty incoming /
     outgoing edges and used_candidate_free_prepare_fast_path = true.

   - Read set empty, write set non-empty. We still build the active
     HandleView snapshot and query
     ActiveEdgeDiscoveryIndex::incoming_candidate_refs +
     registry.committed_reader_candidates against the write_key_summary.
     If BOTH candidate lists are empty, we return the same
     candidate-free fast path (no edges, no DRO evaluation). Otherwise
     we call the full discover_incoming_edges +
     evaluate_prepare_t3_dro and commit-pivot check, but with
     outgoing_edges left empty because we know the read set is empty
     so no out-RW edges exist. The final PreparedConcurrentCommit
     reflects has_in_rw from the discovered incoming edges with
     has_out_rw=false, plus used_candidate_free_prepare_fast_path=false
     to distinguish from the trivial case.

   finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi() adds a matching
   finalize fast-path: when incoming+outgoing edges are empty and the
   read/write keys are empty, it batches the commit index, releases
   the held-lock set, and marks the handle committed without walking
   the edge-apply machinery.

2. invariants.rs: drop the record_cas_attempt call from publish().
   Since 2f2aecb0 switched the chain-head install to a strong
   compare_exchange — and the acquire_chain_head_slot single-writer
   invariant proves no concurrent publisher can race on the slot —
   the attempt count is provably always 1. Recording a histogram
   sample that's a compile-time constant is pure hot-path overhead
   with no observability value. The record_cas_attempt function
   stays in crate::observability for the test harness and any
   future lock-free installer path that legitimately retries; the
   module-level comment above the removed import documents why
   publish does not call it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
